key_findings: Agent A (Technical Moat): Credo owns 100+ issued US patents on HiWire AEC and 224G SerDes technology validated by successful ITC enforcement action and March 2026 settlements with Molex and TE Connectivity. The company is 2-3 years ahead of all competitors (Marvell, Broadcom, Astera Labs) and has achieved production-scale qualification with all 5 major hyperscalers — Microsoft, Amazon/AWS, xAI, Google, and Meta.

Agent B (Financial Analysis): Revenue has grown from $32M in Q4 FY2023 to $407M in Q3 FY2026 (+1,169%), with gross margin expanding from 57.9% to 68.5% over the same period. The company beat its own guidance midpoint in 10 of 11 completed quarters with an average recent beat of +10-17%. Net cash position stands at $1.29B with FCF of $140M in Q3 FY2026 alone. Q4 FY2026 guided at $425-435M; FY2027 guided at >50% YoY growth.

Agent C (Market Sizing): Big-4 hyperscaler combined capex is surging from $359B (2025) to $650B (2026). The data-center-specific AEC market is growing from $296M (2024) to an estimated $2.1B by 2028 at 23.8% CAGR. Credo holds 75% AEC market share today. Bottom-up TAM model implies Credo-addressable revenue of ~$1.3B in FY2026 growing to ~$2.5B in FY2028. Goldman Sachs projects $4-5B long-term AEC revenue potential.

Agent D (Alternative & Sentiment Data): 17 analysts cover CRDO with 15 Buy/2 Hold/0 Sell consensus and a median price target of $200 (vs. $101.45 current price, implying ~97% upside). Short interest at 6.1% of float with days-to-cover of only 1.4. Options IV at ~80% in the 33rd percentile — options are historically cheap. Institutional ownership at 80.46% with net inflows. AEC patent litigation resolved via Molex and TE Connectivity settlements in March 2026, removing a key legal overhang.
top_risks: Risk 1: Customer concentration — Top 3 hyperscalers represent 88% of Q3 FY2026 revenue (39% + 32% + 17%). Any single hyperscaler pause, pivot to CPO, or qualification delay creates outsized revenue risk.

Risk 2: Marvell AEC competitive entry — Marvell launched its 'Golden Cable' initiative (Dec 2025) and demonstrated 800G/1.6T AECs at SC25. Best-case production ramp 2027-2028, still 2-3 years behind Credo, but potential for market share erosion from 75% toward 60-65% by 2027.

Risk 3: Margin compression and valuation risk — Q4 FY2026 gross margin guided at 64-66% vs Q3's 68.6%, driven by ZeroFlap optical ramp product mix. Stock trades at ~55x trailing P/E; if FY2027 growth prints at only 30-40% vs. guided 50%+, significant multiple compression risk at current $101.45 price.

segments.name: Product Engineering Services
segments.description: Custom engineering services for hyperscaler qualification and integration

moat.technical_explanation: Credo's moat rests on three pillars: (1) Proprietary 224G PAM4 SerDes IP on TSMC N3 process — at 56 GHz Nyquist, channel loss can reach 40-50 dB in typical rack environments; Credo's embedded DSP with CDR+re-equalization enables 3-7m reach over 32 AWG twinax that is physically impossible with passive DAC. (2) ZeroFlap zero-link-flap reliability — 1,000x better than optical modules per management due to no laser components; critical for hyperscaler uptime SLAs. (3) HiWire system-level integration — Credo designs not just the chip but the complete cable assembly, co-designing with hyperscalers since 2019-2021, creating sticky customer relationships and switching costs.
moat.qualification_timeline: Qualification Phase 1 (Design Win): 12-18 months of signal integrity testing. Phase 2 (Engineering Validation): 6-12 months of system integration. Phase 3 (Production Validation): 3-6 months at scale. Phase 4 (Volume Ramp): Full production. Microsoft qualified 2021, Amazon qualified 2022-2023, xAI 2024, Google 2025-2026, Meta 2025-2026. Total pipeline ~3-4 year head start vs. new entrants.

competitor_status.marvell: Sampling stage only. Announced 'Golden Cable' initiative Dec 2025; Foxconn demonstrated 800G/1.6T AECs at SC25. No confirmed hyperscaler volume production. Entering qualification cycles Credo began in 2019-2021. Best-case volume ramp 2027-2028, ~3-4 years behind Credo.
competitor_status.broadcom: Announced 'industry first' 800G AEC retimer (7m+ reach) March 2025 via Sian3 3nm DSP. Primary focus is optical transceivers, not copper AEC. CEO Hock Tan (March 2026) stated copper sufficient through 2028 — implicitly validating Credo's market. No confirmed hyperscaler AEC design wins.
competitor_status.astera_labs: Competing in AEC paddle card / NIC-to-ToR market. Primarily focused on PCIe/CXL retimers. 5-10% estimated AEC market share and growing. Deutsche Bank estimates AEC modules ~$3B by 2027; Astera is better positioned for PCIe AEC than HiWire-style deployments.
competitor_status.hyperscaler_internal: No confirmed case of a hyperscaler developing its own AEC silicon. Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) is the primary internal push — SemiAnalysis projects CPO begins production 2026-2027, representing a longer-term displacement risk but also a market Credo is entering via ZeroFlap optics.

key_signals: 9 optical engineering roles in Ottawa — ZeroFlap optics ramp is real and accelerating
7 ASIC/SerDes roles including Director ASIC Design and Program Manager SerDes
2 new Sales Director roles: Neocloud/Enterprise AI and Enterprise OEM (HPE/Dell/Lenovo) — expanding beyond hyperscalers

q2_fy2027_setup.timing_note: Q4 FY2026 reports June 2, 2026. Q1 FY2027 (fiscal quarter ending ~late July 2026) reports September 2026. The 'August 2026' catalyst likely refers to Q1 FY2027 results.
q2_fy2027_setup.fy2027_management_guidance: >50% YoY growth from FY2026 ~$1.3B base; mid-single-digit sequential growth starting Q1 FY2027
q2_fy2027_setup.thesis_consensus_234m_note: The $234M figure is stale — predates the massive FY2026 beat-and-raise sequence. Current run-rate is ~$430M+ per quarter.
q2_fy2027_setup.bull_case: ZeroFlap optics ramp adds $50-100M+ incremental per quarter in H2 FY2027. AEC 200G/lane transition drives ASP uplift. PCIe Gen6 retimers convert to revenue. FY2027 revenue could reach $2.1B+ (60%+ YoY growth).
q2_fy2027_setup.bear_case: Hyperscaler customer pause, ZeroFlap optical margin dilution continues below 65%, Marvell accelerated ramp, tariff headwinds on China operations. FY2027 growth at 30-40% would cause significant multiple compression.

insiders.assessment: CTO sold ~$122M, COO ~$91M, CEO ~$49M in last 6 months — likely via pre-planned 10b5-1 plans. No open-market purchases. Notable but not unusual for executives with large equity comp in stock up 137% over prior year. Overall insider stake at ~10.4% remains substantial and long-term aligned.

key_moat_factors: Proprietary low-power SerDes on 28nm/3nm (vs. competitors' more expensive nodes)
ZeroFlap reliability — 1,000x better than optical modules
Expanding product portfolio: PCIe Gen6 AECs, Blue Heron 224G retimer, ZF Optics, OmniConnect

next_gen_448g.next_gen: 448G bidirectional (NVLink 6, Vera Rubin NVL72) — effectively 448G aggregate per lane pair
next_gen_448g.vera_rubin_key_change: Doubles scale-out NICs per GPU (2x CX-9 per GPU = 1.6T), doubles NIC-to-ToR AEC demand per rack. Same copper backplane cable count but 2x bandwidth via bidirectional SerDes.
next_gen_448g.credo_positioning: Blue Heron 224G multiprotocol retimer (UALink, ESUN, Ethernet) on 3nm — sampling now, production Q3 CY2026. 1.6T ZeroFlap AECs sampling, production ramp H2 CY2026.
next_gen_448g.rationale: Vera Rubin doubles NIC-to-ToR AEC demand per rack; 1.6T AECs command higher ASPs; Blue Heron opens new scale-UP backplane TAM; Rubin Ultra (H2 2027) drives next upgrade cycle.
